column4: Chain of Command's own crypt-return condition when its temporary vampires can't bleed; fails transfer — no other card shares this specific requirement-then-return mechanic. Nearest 6.2 (temporary control) but that section doesn't own this return condition.

Card-specific clarification of what Chameleon may target (own vampires, to grant Anarch sect); fails transfer — answers only this card's own targeting legality. Nearest 5.1 (vampire trait changes), which doesn't own targeting scope.

mandatory actions: a minion whose mandatory action is prevented is stuck and cannot act instead. No section owns "mandatory action" as a concept; nearest 5.7 (special minion traits, since mandatory actions are usually trait-driven, e.g. werewolves).
!DROPPED:1.8 the "stays in hand if canceled/failed" clause also touches 1.8's cancellation scope.

Charming Lobby's own consequence of {Delaying Tactics} (card returns to hand, others barred from the action); fails transfer — the "others forbidden" clause is this card's printed text, not a general rule. Nearest 3.5 (canceled/failed action consequences).
Charnas' own trigger condition (does nothing to an empty vampire that unlocks-in-torpor); fails transfer — specific to Charnas' blood-emptiness check. Nearest 5.3 (torpor state).

Card-specific note on which counter types Church of the Order of St. Blaise may place on locations; fails transfer — no general principle about counters, just this card's compatibility. Nearest 1.12 (cards on cards / counters on hosts).
sect as a trait: clan-change effects do not change sect. Confirms watch-list gap (nothing owns sect itself, only sect change via titles/traits). Nearest 5.1 (vampire trait changes).

competing continuous modifications of the same trait (clan): most recent effect takes precedence. No section owns general effect-precedence/layering rules for stacked continuous modifications. Nearest 5.1 (clan changes) and 2.4 (ordering).
!DROPPED:5.4 "does not burn if temporarily out of play" also touches 5.4 (burning minions).
actions "requiring an Anarch"/"requiring a Baron" are sect/title-linked action-requirement categories; nothing owns them (relates to watch-list "sect as a trait"). Nearest 5.8 (titles).

Restates that this strike-type effect only functions at close range, per the cited rulebook resolve-strike rule; fails non-obviousness — a judge fluent in the rulebook already knows this. Nearest 4.2 (range).
Card-specific numeric-allocation constraint of Conservative Agitation's point distribution; fails transfer — no other card shares this exact allocation clause. Nearest 3.7.5 (referendum procedure).
Restates that you may allocate more points than a target has pool, mirroring the well-known bleed-for-more-than-pool rule; fails non-obviousness. Nearest 6.5 (pool).

Confirms Consignment to Duat may target a vampire with no blood; fails non-obviousness — targeting "a vampire" never implicitly requires blood > 0 absent card text saying so. Nearest 1.6 (requirements).
Card-specific clan requirement (must not control a ready Nagaraja) gating Consume the Dead's burn option; fails transfer — specific to this card's own printed condition. Nearest 1.6 (requirements).

Reminder that Coterie Tactics does not override the normal prey/predator/target restrictions; fails non-obviousness — restrictions apply by default absent an overriding card text. Nearest 3.1 (targets).
Card-specific requirement gating Council of Seraphim's burn option (all Seraph in torpor); fails transfer — specific to this card's own group of vampires. Nearest 5.3 (torpor state).

cumulative/stacking effects: multiple copies of the same named ability (Courier) can each act in succession since the "top" card changes between resolutions. Confirms watch-list gap (nothing owns stacking of identical instances). Nearest 1.2 (periodicity).

Restates that an "or" clan requirement (Tremere or Tremere antitribu) is satisfied by either; fails non-obviousness — reading "or" in a requirement clause is the ordinary meaning. Nearest 1.6 (requirements).
same gap as Club Illusion.1 (R0367): action "requiring a Baron" implies "requiring an Anarch" — a title-to-sect requirement link nothing owns. Nearest 5.8 (titles).
Card-specific restriction (unusable if the action that triggered it also ousted you); fails transfer — tied to this card's own printed condition. Nearest 6.5 (ousting).
same mandatory-action gap as Change of Target.8 (R0323): a minion free to act normally once its mandatory action is discharged. Nearest 5.7.
